channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Oil prices rise sharply in market trading after attacks in Middle East disrupt supply
item.description: The high prices came as U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes against Israel and U.S. military installations around the Gulf sent disruptions through the global energy supply chain.
item.content:encoded:
The high prices came as U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes against Israel and U.S. military installations around the Gulf sent disruptions through the global energy supply chain.

(Image credit: Kamran Jebreili)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Satellite images provide view inside Iran at war
item.description: Satellite images from commercial companies show the extent of U.S. and Israeli strikes, and how Iran is responding.
item.content:encoded: Satellite images from commercial companies show the extent of U.S. and Israeli strikes, and how Iran is responding.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: Photos: U.S.-Israeli strikes in Iran and reactions from around the world
item.description: Here's a look at Iran, Israel and reactions from around the world after the U.S. and Israel launched strikes against Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Here's a look at Iran, Israel and reactions from around the world after the U.S. and Israel launched strikes against Iran.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Nickolai Hammar

item.title: Mideast clashes breach Olympic truce as athletes gather for Winter Paralympic Games
item.description: Fighting intensified in the Middle East during the Olympic truce, in effect through March 15. Flights are being disrupted as athletes and families converge on Italy for the Winter Paralympics.
item.content:encoded:
Fighting intensified in the Middle East during the Olympic truce, in effect through March 15. Flights are being disrupted as athletes and families converge on Italy for the Winter Paralympics.

(Image credit: Luca Bruno)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: A U.S. scholarship thrills a teacher in India. Then came the soul-crushing questions
item.description: She was thrilled to become the first teacher from a government-sponsored school in India to get a Fulbright exchange award to learn from U.S. schools. People asked two questions that clouded her joy.
item.content:encoded:
She was thrilled to become the first teacher from a government-sponsored school in India to get a Fulbright exchange award to learn from U.S. schools. People asked two questions that clouded her joy.

(Image credit: Anupam Gangopadhyay)
item.dc:creator: Joyeeta Banerjee

item.title: 3 American troops killed, and Trump says more 'likely,' in war against Iran
item.description: The deaths of three U.S. service members mark the first American casualties since the start of operation "Epic Fury" on Saturday. President Trump said "there will likely be more" Americans killed.
item.content:encoded:
The deaths of three U.S. service members mark the first American casualties since the start of operation "Epic Fury" on Saturday. President Trump said "there will likely be more" Americans killed.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Trump warns Iran not to retaliate after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is killed
item.description: The Iranian government has announced 40 days of mourning. The country's supreme leader was killed following an attack launched by the U.S. and Israel on Saturday against Iran.
item.content:encoded:
The Iranian government has announced 40 days of mourning. The country's supreme leader was killed following an attack launched by the U.S. and Israel on Saturday against Iran.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Explosion rocks Iran's capital as Israel says it is targeting the city
item.description: Iran fired missiles at targets in Israel and Gulf Arab states Sunday after vowing massive retaliation for the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei by the United States and Israel.
item.content:encoded:
Iran fired missiles at targets in Israel and Gulf Arab states Sunday after vowing massive retaliation for the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei by the United States and Israel.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How some Iranians reacted to attacks on their country
item.description: What are people in Iran saying about today's attacks by the U.S. and Israel? We hear from people on the ground who experienced the event as it unfolded.
item.content:encoded: What are people in Iran saying about today's attacks by the U.S. and Israel? We hear from people on the ground who experienced the event as it unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is killed in Israeli strike, ending 36-year iron rule
item.description: Khamenei, the Islamic Republic's second supreme leader, has been killed. He had held power since 1989, guiding Iran through difficult times — and overseeing the violent suppression of dissent.
item.content:encoded:
Khamenei, the Islamic Republic's second supreme leader, has been killed. He had held power since 1989, guiding Iran through difficult times — and overseeing the violent suppression of dissent.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam
